mission.title: Install and Configure Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK)
mission.description: Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes (ECK) automates the deployment and management of the Elastic Stack on Kubernetes. It simplifies the orchestration of Elasticsearch, Kibana, and other Elastic products, making it ideal for users looking to leverage Elastic's capabilities in a Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in managing custom configurations.

steps.title: Add the ECK Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Elastic Cloud on Kubernetes Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add elastic https://helm.elastic.co

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official Elastic Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the ECK operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ECK operator in the 'elastic-system' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace elastic-system

helm install eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the ECK operator.
> Installs the ECK operator with a specific version.

steps.title: Create the Custom Resource (CR) for Elasticsearch
steps.description:
This step creates a Custom Resource definition for deploying an Elasticsearch cluster.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: fs
EOF

```

> This command creates an Elasticsearch cluster with 3 nodes.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: fs
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Elasticsearch deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Elasticsearch cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

kubectl describe elasticsearch my-elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> This command lists the Elasticsearch resources in the 'elastic-system' namespace.
> Describes the Elasticsearch resource for detailed status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the ECK operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ECK operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Elasticsearch clusters and any data stored within them.

```bash

helm uninstall eck-operator --namespace elastic-system

kubectl delete namespace elastic-system

```

> Uninstalls the ECK operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the ECK operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Elasticsearch data. This step upgrades the ECK operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o json > elasticsearch-backup.json

helm upgrade eck-operator elastic/eck-operator --namespace elastic-system --version 2.14.1

kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system

```

> Backs up the current Elasticsearch configuration.
> Upgrades the ECK operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Elasticsearch resources are still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system -o json > elasticsearch-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Elasticsearch cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Elasticsearch cluster may not have started correctly due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation and ensure that the nodes have enough CPU and memory.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Kibana'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kibana may not be deployed or is not reachable due to network policies.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Kibana resource is created and check network policies that may block access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Custom Resource definition is not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The ECK operator may not be installed or the CRD may not be applied.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the ECK operator and ensure CRDs are applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to access resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** RBAC settings may not allow the operator to manage resources.

**Fix:**
Check and update the RBAC roles and bindings for the ECK operator.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Elasticsearch 9.x, improved TLS certificate management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.12 or 2.13, ensure all Elasticsearch clusters are compatible with version 9.x.

resolution.summary: You should see the Elasticsearch cluster status as 'green' when running `kubectl get elasticsearch -n elastic-system`.
codeSnippets:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: elasticsearch.k8s.elastic.co/v1
kind: Elasticsearch
metadata:
  name: my-elasticsearch
  namespace: elastic-system
spec:
  version: 8.5.0
  nodeSets:
  - name: default
    count: 3
    config:
      node.store.type: fs
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
